survivors_flagged.one_line: Canvuparatide hypoPT: Ph2 hit primary; 1-yr OLE data + EOP2/Ph3-init dated Q2-Q3 2026 — real dated milestones but development-path (dilutive), not a mispriced approval binary.
Revascor BLA on RMAT accelerated-approval path (filed late-2025, FDA-aligned on CMC/endpoint); decision pending 2026 but no firm PDUFA date confirmed.
Brook Mine rare-earth (Nd/Pr/Dy/Tb) pre-feasibility study + pilot production expected 2026, DOE CRADA + REalloys offtake MOU; real shortage-moat angle but soft (year-not-day) timing.
Live $18B/$48.30-cash take-private proposal from Diller's People Inc. (June 1 2026); merger-arb, Diller already owns 26.1% and recuses on board, unresolved.
Midera food-processing spin completes July 6 2026 (record date June 26, MFP regular-way July 7, tax-free 1:1); imminent dated sum-of-parts unlock.
Zilurgisertib FOP NDA on Priority Review, PDUFA Sept 26 2026 + multiple Ph3 readouts H2'26-Q1'27 (volixibat/brelovitug); dated binaries on a profitable rare-disease base.
Sonelokimab HS BLA filing end-Sep-2026 + PDUFA notice by end-Nov-2026, but pivotal Phase 3 VELA readouts already fired/re-rated; filing-step not a binary.
Lorundrostat NDA accepted; hard PDUFA Dec-22-2026 in resistant hypertension — dated regulatory binary with $646M cash, asymmetry intact.
Lennar-spun land-banking REIT with a live pending deal: financing (Goldman ~$500M confidence letter) for Dream Finders/Beazer homesite acquisition — dated, forward, not yet closed.
Board-approved tax-free spin splitting Knicks from Rangers; Form 10 filed with SEC May-2026 (~$13.5B SOTP), classic Dolan-discount unlock pending Form 10 effectiveness + league approval.
Owns big McEwen Copper stake; targeted Oct-Dec'26 McEwen Copper IPO + Los Azules FID year-end'26 crystallizes sum-of-parts.
Myers Tire Supply divestiture (in discontinued ops) expected to close 2026 — transformative but small portfolio reshape.
Obicetrapib EMA/UK/Swiss decisions expected 2H'26 (via Menarini) — dated regulatory binary but EU-only, US path unclear.
Announced Brink's takeover ($6.6B: $30 cash + 0.1574 BCO/sh), close Q1'27 — live cash+stock merger-arb spread.
Elk Creek bankability binary: ~$780M financing targeted Q2'26 close + Stellantis definitive RE supply agreement + DoD DPA milestones.

The sole remaining dated event is a Phase 3 pivotal trial start in Q3 2026, which is a dilutive development/cash-burn milestone, not a mispriced unpriced value binary, and no EOP2 FDA meeting date is disclosed. Fails all Bloom gates; the catalyst is fired and re-rated, so NONE.

verified.catalyst: FDA accelerated-approval decision on Revascor (rexlemestrocel-L) RMAT-designated BLA for end-stage ischemic HFrEF patients with an LVAD.
verified.dated_milestone: BLA filing targeted year-end 2025 under RMAT; accelerated-approval decision expected in 2026 — but no firm PDUFA action date publicly confirmed as of June 2026 (next milestone = FDA acceptance/PDUFA assignment).
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (FDA-aligned concrete commitment on CMC, potency assays, and confirmatory-trial endpoint/accelerated pathway at the June 3, 2025 Type B meeting)
verified.edge: M — RMAT + FDA-stated "studies could support accelerated approval" is a genuine binary regulatory event for a sub-$ small cap, but the lack of a confirmed PDUFA/acceptance date and unverified actual filing status cap conviction; outcome and timing both still uncertain.
verified.primary_source: Mesoblast/GlobeNewswire press release "Mesoblast and FDA Align on Key Items for Revascor BLA in Ischemic Heart Failure" (2025-07-01); FDA June 3, 2025 Type B meeting minutes referenced therein.
verified.analysis: Revascor's RMAT-designated accelerated-approval BLA in LVAD-implanted end-stage ischemic HFrEF is a real, specific, forward binary catalyst: at the June 3, 2025 Type B meeting the FDA aligned with Mesoblast on CMC, commercial-release potency assays, and the post-approval confirmatory-trial endpoint, and stated the presented data could support accelerated approval. Company guidance was to file by YE2025 with a potential 2026 decision, but I could not confirm a firm PDUFA action date or formal BLA acceptance in the public record as of June 2026 — the most recent specific dated milestone remains the mid-2025 alignment, which is the verification gap. Passes real+dated+forward+non-macro gating, but the missing PDUFA/acceptance confirmation holds it at WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Revised Hatch conceptual study (carbochlorination flowsheet) due late June 2026, followed by Weir International Technical Report Summary, both guided to show materially higher revenue/FCF vs the July-2025 Fluor PEA for the Brook Mine REE/critical-minerals deposit.
verified.dated_milestone: Revised Hatch conceptual study ~late June 2026; Weir TRS "soon thereafter"; next: PFS H2-2026; pilot plant building complete late summer/fall 2026, pilot operations slipped to 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: carbochlorination upgrade guided to materially increase revenue/FCF vs the published $1.2B-NPV8 / 38%-IRR Fluor PEA); G1/G2 weak — REalloys MOU (May 28 2026) is explicitly NON-BINDING with no quantified volume in METC's release, DOE/CRADA only a research collaboration not an offtake commitment.
verified.edge: L — real US rare-earth supply-shortage moat (largest unconventional coal-hosted Nd/Pr/Dy/Tb deposit, Fluor-confirmed feasibility), but METC is already a heavily re-rated DOE/REE momentum name so most optionality is priced; the near-term catalyst is a self-prepared study revision, not a hard external commitment, and the headline PEA is already fired.
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire "Ramaco Retains Hatch to Lead Pre-Feasibility Study for Brook Mine REE/CM Project" + Ramaco Q1 2026 results (May 11 2026) citing late-June Hatch study / Weir TRS; REalloys MOU PRNewswire (May 28 2026, non-binding)
verified.analysis: METC's Brook Mine is a legitimate domestic rare-earth shortage play, but the marquee value-driver (Fluor PEA: $1.2B NPV8, 38% IRR, July 2025) is already published and priced, and the original mid-2026 pilot-production timing in the triage note has slipped to 2027. The genuine remaining dated forward catalyst is the late-June-2026 revised Hatch conceptual study plus a Weir International TRS on the new carbochlorination flowsheet, which management guides to a material revenue/FCF uplift over the Fluor base case (G3) — but it is a company-commissioned study, the REalloys offtake is non-binding with no quantified volume, and the milestone is essentially imminent (today is June 29), so the unpriced window is narrow. Net: real but soft — WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Barry Diller's People Inc. (26.1% holder) made a June 1, 2026 non-binding $48.30/share ($18B) all-cash proposal to take MGM Resorts private; the board is reviewing and no agreement is reached.
verified.dated_milestone: MGM board/special-committee response to the June 1 proposal; no firm date set — next checkpoint likely the Q2 2026 earnings call (~late July/early Aug 2026) or a definitive-merger 8-K/13D/A.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: People Inc./Diller) + G3 (specific $48.30/$18B figure) PASS; G2 fails — proposal is explicitly non-binding with no board commitment.
verified.edge: L — stock $49.69 trades ~2.9% ABOVE the $48.30 cash offer (negative arb spread), so the tape has already priced in and exceeded the bid; from yearLow $29.19 / 200d $36.77 it is fully re-rated. No unpriced edge left at the proposal price; remaining upside is a speculative bump, not a discount-to-deal.
verified.primary_source: SEC Schedule 13D/A & IAC/People Inc. 8-K (PRNewswire), filed June 1, 2026; MGM quote $49.69 (FMP, 2026-06-29); BFA Law investigation alert (Morningstar/BusinessWire, 2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The June 1, 2026 take-private proposal is real, named, and dated ($48.30/share cash, $18B, Diller's People Inc. already owns 26.1% and recuses), and remains unresolved (BFA Law opened a fiduciary-process investigation June 29). But MGM closed at $49.69, ~2.9% above the offer, so the market is pricing a higher/sweetened bid rather than offering a positive merger-arb spread — the catalyst is effectively fired and re-rated, not forward-unpriced. The G3 "currently-unpriced figure" test fails at the $48.30 level, so this is a WATCH for a definitive deal/board response, not an ACTIVE entry.

verified.catalyst: Middleby completes the tax-free 1:1 spin-off of Midera Food Processing (Nasdaq: MFP) on July 6, 2026, mechanically separating a ~$172M-EBITDA standalone processor and a 49% Composition Brands stake from the ~$627M-EBITDA Commercial Foodservice RemainCo.
verified.dated_milestone: Distribution effective July 6, 2026 (record date June 26 passed); MFP regular-way trading begins July 7, 2026
verified.instrument: MIDD common (capture pre-distribution; post-spin holders receive 1 MFP per MIDD share)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (Nasdaq MFP listing + defined distribution mechanics, distribution agent/Form 10 effective June 17) and G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: Midera $172M 2025 adj EBITDA → $1.17B-$2.15B SoP equity value being distributed; RemainCo + Composition Brands stake ~ pre-spin value, implying Midera "for free")
verified.edge: M — clean, well-telegraphed tax-free spin announced Feb 2025 with a Form 10 effective since June 17, so the SoP rerating is partly priced; the genuine, tradable edge is the dated 1-week-out mechanical unlock and the post-spin re-rating of the pure-play processor plus any forced-selling dislocation in MFP on July 7.
verified.primary_source: Middleby Board of Directors Approves Spin-off of Midera Food Processing — Businesswire, 2026-06-22; corroborated by SEC 8-Ks (MIDD CIK 0000769520 / Midera CIK 0002088281) and stockspinoffs.com 2026-06-01
verified.kill_fact: Independently verified vs June 22 board-approval release + SEC 8-Ks (data.sec.gov filing index 000119312526276741/276742) + Form 10 effectiveness: tax-free 1:1 distribution of Midera (Nasdaq: MFP), record date June 26 2026, distribution July 6 2026 12:01 a.m. ET, regular-way MFP trading July 7. Magnitudes confirmed via independent SoP: Midera ~$850M rev / $172M (2025) EBITDA, ~1.25x leverage, $1.17B-$2.15B equity; RemainCo $627M EBITDA + 49% Composition Brands stake (~$434M + $135M note). MIDD ~$173.81 on June 29 2026 — forward, not yet fired (distribution 7 days out). Score held at 7 (not higher) because the breakup has been signaled/anticipated for a long time and the source itself says MIDD 'likely trades at a meaningful discount,' so part of the conglomerate-discount close may already be priced. Minor non-material nuances: when-issued tickers MFPVV/MIDDV from June 26 (prompt omitted); RemainCo revenue ~$2.4B. No hidden disqualifier; large-cap, liquid, US-listed, fully tradeable pre-distribution.
verified.analysis: The catalyst is fully verified against the June 22 board-approval release and SEC 8-Ks: tax-free 1:1 distribution of Midera (MFP), record date June 26, distribution July 6, regular-way trading July 7, 2026 — dated, forward, and not yet fired as of June 29. Midera is a ~$850M-revenue / ~$172M-EBITDA standalone (~1.25x leverage at separation) carrying $1.17B-$2.15B of SoP equity value, while RemainCo Middleby plus its 49% Composition Brands stake (~$434M + $135M note) is argued to be worth roughly the full pre-spin price, i.e. shareholders effectively receive Midera for free. It is not a macro bet; the main discount to a higher score is that the breakup has been signaled since early 2025, so a meaningful portion of the conglomerate-discount close may already be in the tape.

verified.catalyst: Stacked dated binaries on a profitable rare-disease base: zilurgisertib FOP NDA on FDA Priority Review (PDUFA Sept 26, 2026, partnered with Incyte) plus brelovitug HDV Phase 3 (AZURE-1/AZURE-4) and volixibat PSC NDA, all hitting H2'26.
verified.dated_milestone: Zilurgisertib FOP PDUFA decision — September 26, 2026 (next); brelovitug AZURE Ph3 topline + volixibat PSC NDA submission, both H2 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete dated FDA PDUFA commitment Sept 26 2026 + completed Ph3 enrollment with H2'26 topline); G1 (named counterparty Incyte, worldwide license April 2026)
verified.edge: M — multiple genuinely-forward dated catalysts (PDUFA approval decision + 2 Ph3 readouts) on a cash-generative commercial base de-risks the binary, but the positive FOP Phase 2 data already printed at ENDO (June 14 2026) and the stock has run from $47.89 to ~$123 (near 52-wk high), so much of the easy re-rate is already priced and the name is heavily followed.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan/Businesswire, "Zilurgisertib FOP data to debut at ENDO 2026 before Sept. 26 FDA PDUFA date" (June 4, 2026); Mirum Q1 2026 results / business update (May 6, 2026)
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY (Mirum Q1'26 IR release / 8-K) confirms the forward binaries: FDA accepted zilurgisertib FOP NDA under Priority Review, PDUFA Sept 26 2026; brelovitug AZURE-1/AZURE-4 Ph3 HDV enrollment complete, topline H2'26; volixibat PSC NDA submission H2'26; Q1'26 net product sales $159.9M; FY26 guidance raised $660-680M; GAAP net loss $790.2M driven by a one-time $726.3M Bluejay IPR&D charge (operational base profitable). CORRECTIONS: (1) the Incyte deal is an IN-license (Mirum licensed worldwide rights FROM Incyte, $16M upfront + royalties TO Incyte) — 'partnered with Incyte' is directionally right but the 'April 2026' date is unconfirmed by the primary release. (2) +43% YoY is unverified; LIVMARLI grew +55%. SKEPTIC FLAG (already noted in line): the FOP PROGRESS Ph2 data is FIRED (presented ENDO June 14 2026, 99.9% reduction in new bone lesions) and largely priced — stock ~tripled off $47.89 low to all-time high $124.44 (June 26 2026), now trading near/above the analyst MIN target ($115). What remains forward = the approval DECISION plus two Ph3 readouts, not new de-risking data.
verified.analysis: FDA accepted the zilurgisertib FOP NDA under Priority Review with a confirmed PDUFA date of Sept 26, 2026, and the asset is partnered with Incyte (worldwide license, April 2026) — a real, dated, forward, un-fired approval binary. Two more dated catalysts stack into H2'26: brelovitug HDV Phase 3 (AZURE-1/AZURE-4 enrollment complete, topline H2'26) and a volixibat PSC NDA submission, all on a profitable commercial base (Q1'26 revenue $159.9M +43% YoY, FY26 guidance raised to $660-680M; the GAAP loss is a one-time Bluejay IPR&D charge, not operational). Caveat: the positive FOP Phase 2 PROGRESS data already presented at ENDO on June 14 2026 and the stock has roughly tripled off its 52-wk low to near its high, so the data-readout leg is fired and partly priced — what remains forward is the approval decision itself plus the two Ph3 readouts.

The remaining Sep/Nov-2026 events are administrative filing/acceptance steps, not unpriced binaries; the real next binary is the FDA approval decision (~2027 PDUFA action date), not a 2026 catalyst.

verified.analysis: The pivotal VELA Phase 3 readouts (Week 16/40/52) and the June-22-2026 Investor Day have all fired and the stock has fully re-rated — MLTX trades at $19.91 near its 52-week low ($5.95-$62.75 range), reflecting the VELA-2 Week-16 primary-endpoint miss (no statistical significance, high placebo arm) alongside the VELA-1 win. The end-Sep-2026 BLA submission and end-Nov-2026 acceptance/PDUFA-date notification are dated and forward but are procedural filing steps, not the value-determining binary; the genuine unfired binary (FDA approval decision) sits in ~2027. verified.catalyst: FDA decision on lorundrostat NDA for adults with uncontrolled/resistant hypertension — a hard dated approval-vs-CRL binary.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA target action date Dec-22-2026 (NDA accepted Mar-9-2026, standard 10-month review)
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (FDA-accepted NDA with assigned action date = concrete scheduled commitment); G1 partial (Tanabe Pharma is a named license counterparty, but that is dev/commercial licensing, not the catalyst itself)
verified.edge: M — known, heavily-covered catalyst (8 Buys, avg PT ~$52 already implies ~2x), so approval is partly anticipated; but the binary outcome and label breadth remain genuinely unpriced at a $1.78B cap trading below 50d/200d MAs with $646M cash floor, so asymmetry survives.
verified.primary_source: Mineralys IR press release "FDA Acceptance of NDA for Lorundrostat" + StockTitan, Mar-9-2026; price FMP quote ~Jun-2026
verified.kill_fact: Primary-source verified (Mineralys IR press releases): lorundrostat NDA accepted Mar-9-2026, PDUFA target action date Dec-22-2026 for adults with hypertension in combination with other antihypertensives — a forward, not-yet-fired dated binary (~6 months ahead of today 2026-06-29). Cash/equivalents/investments $646.1M as of Mar-31-2026, runway into 2028 (funds through launch); five positive Phase 2/3 trials (Launch-HTN, Advance-HTN, etc.). Two triage corrections CONFIRMED: (1) label is broader 'adults with hypertension,' not resistant-only — initial launch focus is resistant but the proposed indication is broader uncontrolled HTN; (2) STANDARD review, not priority. Edge is medium not high: event is well-telegraphed and consensus already prices substantial upside, so it is a clean asymmetric binary but not mispriced. Score 7/ACTIVE upheld.
verified.analysis: Verified: Mineralys's lorundrostat NDA was accepted by FDA on Mar-9-2026 with a firm PDUFA action date of Dec-22-2026 for adults with hypertension (uncontrolled/resistant) in combination with other antihypertensives — a dated, forward, not-yet-fired regulatory binary backed by five positive trials and $646.1M cash (runway into 2028, funds through a launch). One correction to the triage: the proposed label is broader uncontrolled hypertension, not strictly resistant-only, and it is standard (not priority) review. Score 7 / ACTIVE: clean dated asymmetric binary with a hard cash floor, tempered because the event is well-telegraphed and consensus already prices substantial upside, leaving the edge medium rather than high.

verified.catalyst: Millrose's $500M Goldman "highly confident" letter to land-bank Beazer homesites is contingent on Dream Finders' acquisition of Beazer closing — but that bid is hostile, rejected three times, and has no agreement or close date.
verified.dated_milestone: None — no definitive Dream Finders/Beazer agreement exists; no close date disclosed (proposals Feb 5 / Mar 17 / May 5 2026 all rejected by Beazer's board).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 partial (named counterparties: Goldman Sachs, Dream Finders, Beazer) and G3 partial ($500M figure, currently unpriced) PRESENT — but G2 FAILS: the Goldman letter is a non-binding "highly confident" letter, not a firm commitment, and is doubly contingent on a board-rejected hostile deal.
verified.edge: L — no dated forward trigger; MRP benefit is contingent-on-a-contingent (Goldman letter conditional on a Dream Finders/Beazer deal that the target's board has rejected three times with a poison pill and no timeline).
verified.primary_source: Millrose press release "Land Banking Capital Support for Dream Finders' Proposed Acquisition of Beazer" (businesswire/stocktitan, 2026-05-11); Beazer "Announces Rejection of Unsolicited Proposals from Dream Finders" (businesswire, 2026-05-11); MRP quote $30.68 (FMP, 2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The Goldman $500M financing is a non-binding "highly confident" letter (announced 2026-05-11), not a firm commitment, and Millrose only acquires the homesites "upon completion" of Dream Finders' acquisition of Beazer. That acquisition is a hostile, three-times-rejected unsolicited proposal ($28.50 Feb / $29.00 Mar / $25.75 May) with no definitive agreement, no tender offer, a 4.95% poison pill, and no disclosed close date — so there is no dated forward milestone to trade. MRP trades at $30.68 (vs $30.47 200-day avg, $36 year-high), confirming the overlay is unpriced but lacks a catalyst clock; this fails the strict DATED+FORWARD+concrete-commitment gate.

verified.catalyst: Dolan-controlled MSG Sports is pursuing a tax-free spin-off separating the NY Rangers from the NY Knicks into two public companies, a classic conglomerate-discount unlock against a ~$13.5B SOTP.
verified.dated_milestone: Confidential Form 10 filed with SEC 2026-05-18 (board-authorized 2026-02-18); next gate = Form 10 effectiveness, then NBA + NHL league approvals, tax opinion, and final board approval — no completion timetable set.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (NBA + NHL named as required approving counterparties), G2 (board-authorized + Form 10 filed, tax-free pro-rata 100% distribution committed), G3 (Sportico SOTP $9.85B Knicks + $3.65B Rangers = $13.5B vs ~$9.7B market cap = ~$3.8B/~39% unpriced gap)
verified.edge: M-L: real, specific, dated structural unlock with named league counterparties and a hard SOTP gap, but the stock has already re-rated hard ($188 52wk-low to $403 now, near high, +41% above 200dma) since the Feb/May catalysts, and completion is open-ended (no set timetable), so much of the discount-close is partially priced and the unfired upside is compressed.
verified.primary_source: MSG Sports / SEC Form 10 press release (businesswire, 2026-05-18) + Sportico "$13.5B Breakup" SOTP (2026); current quote FMP 2026-06-29
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm every load-bearing fact: MSG Sports board unanimously authorized the plan to explore the spin-off on Feb 18, 2026 (Businesswire 20260218343916), and on May 18, 2026 the company filed an initial confidential Form 10 to spin the NY Rangers business OUT of the NY Knicks parent as a tax-free, pro-rata 100% distribution (MSG Sports press release + Sportico 2026 explainer). Completion is conditioned on Form 10 effectiveness, NBA/NHL approval, a tax opinion, and final board approval, with NO timetable. SOTP ~$13.5B (Sportico: Knicks $9.85B + Rangers $3.65B) vs FMP live market cap $9.703B at price $403.03 (2026-06-29) = 39.1% gap above mkt cap — matches the line (Sportico's ~29% figure is the EV basis, a different denominator, not a contradiction). UNFIRED and forward (no spin completed) but materially de-risked/partially priced: price sits AT its 52-wk high $403.03 (yearLow $188.595), well above priceAvg50 $357.52 and priceAvg200 $285.77 — the conglomerate-discount unlock is real but the catalyst has already run ~114% off the low, and the completion path is undated. No FIRED, no date error, no terms/magnitude error, no hidden disqualifier; tradeable NYSE common.
verified.analysis: Verified via MSG Sports/SEC press releases and Sportico (2026-05-18): board unanimously authorized (Feb-18) and a confidential Form 10 was filed (May-18) to spin the Rangers out of the Knicks parent as a tax-free, pro-rata distribution; completion is conditioned on Form 10 effectiveness, NBA and NHL approval, a tax opinion, and final board sign-off, with no timetable announced. SOTP of ~$13.5B (Knicks $9.85B / Rangers $3.65B) sits ~39% above the ~$9.7B market cap (price $403.03, FMP 2026-06-29), the textbook Dolan discount. Gap to a clean ACTIVE-high: the share price has already run from a $188 52-week low to a near-high $403 (well above both moving averages), so the catalyst is genuine and unfired but materially de-risked/partially priced, and the undated completion path tempers conviction.

verified.catalyst: A McEwen Copper IPO (~$300M, preferred Oct–Dec 2026 window) plus a Los Azules final investment decision targeted by year-end 2026 would publicly mark and crystallize MUX's 46.3% McEwen Copper stake (last implied ~$457M, ~43% of MUX's ~$1.07B cap).
verified.dated_milestone: McEwen Copper IPO targeted Oct–Dec 2026; Los Azules FID targeted year-end 2026 (construction early 2027)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named: McEwen Copper IPO / SocGen senior-debt arranger), G2 (concrete: $2.4B loan package + ~$300M IPO + $4B Los Azules capex), G3 (specific unpriced figure: ~$457M implied 46.3% stake vs ~$1.07B MUX cap, to be re-marked by public IPO)
verified.edge: M — the 46.3% stake value is already disclosed (~$457M implied) so partly known, but a public IPO mark + FID would force SoP recognition and break the conglomerate discount; offset by copper-price sensitivity, $4B financing dependency, and Argentina/RIGI execution risk, plus the May-2026 debt-financing news already being digested (stock fell ~6%)
verified.primary_source: McEwen Mining Q4 2025 earnings call + MINING.COM "McEwen Copper seeks $4B" (IPO Oct–Dec 2026, FID year-end 2026); Los Azules Feasibility Study PR dated Oct 7, 2025; stake/value per company 8-K filings (46.3%, ~$457M, Mar 2025)
verified.analysis: MUX holds 46.3% of McEwen Copper, whose sole asset Los Azules is an advanced-stage Argentine copper project (Feasibility Study completed Oct 7, 2025; $2.4B senior debt being arranged by SocGen toward ~$4B total capex). Management has explicitly guided a McEwen Copper IPO in a preferred Oct–Dec 2026 window and a Los Azules FID by year-end 2026 — both forward, dated, idiosyncratic value-unlock events that would publicly mark a stake currently implied at ~$457M (~43% of MUX's ~$1.07B market cap). Genuine catalyst but contingent on debt completion and copper macro, and the financing leg already partly fired in May 2026, so WATCH not ACTIVE.

verified.bloom_gates: None pass cleanly. G1 fail (no named buyer — company won't disclose progress; bids received but no signed agreement). G2 fail (Board approved divestiture process but no concrete signed/dated commitment). G3 fail (only disclosed figures are negative/already-booked impairments: $14.8M net-of-tax, $14.7M goodwill writeoff — no currently-unpriced upside proceeds figure).
verified.edge: L — thesis already re-rated; stock +39.3% on the "focused transformation" since the strategic review, so the divestiture optionality is substantially priced.
verified.primary_source: Myers Industries Q1 2026 results / 10-Q (filed 2026-05-07, discontinued-ops + $14.8M net impairment, bids received, expected close 2026); Seeking Alpha "Upside Isn't Over Just Yet" noting +39.3% surge (2026-06-07).
verified.analysis: The divestiture is real and dated-to-2026 with Board approval and bids received, but it fails the strict gate on every dimension: no named counterparty, no signed agreement, no specific currently-unpriced figure (only negative impairments are disclosed), and only a vague "during 2026" timeline. Crucially the catalyst is largely fired — MYE has already surged +39.3% on the focused-transformation narrative (Seeking Alpha, 2026-06-07), so the portfolio-cleanup re-rating is mostly in the price. As the triage flag itself notes, it is a small portfolio reshape; no forward unpriced edge remains.

verified.catalyst: Obicetrapib has two stacked dated binaries: an EMA/MHRA/Swissmedic marketing-authorization decision in 2H26 (via partner Menarini), and the larger PREVAIL cardiovascular-outcomes trial (~9,500 pts) with interim analysis 4Q2026 and topline 1Q2027 that decides whether the drug earns a CV-outcomes label.
verified.dated_milestone: EMA/MHRA/Swissmedic obicetrapib approval decision in 2H2026; PREVAIL CVOT interim analysis 4Q2026, topline 1Q2027
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: A. Menarini International Licensing, EU commercialization partner who filed the MAAs) + G2 (concrete commitment: EMA-validated MAAs under active review with decision guided to 2H26; PREVAIL interim event-driven 4Q2026)
verified.edge: M — EU approval is partly priced (Phase 3 LDL data BROADWAY/BROOKLYN/TANDEM already positive, low-beta 0.028 reflects a de-risked LDL story), but the PREVAIL CVOT outcome (1Q2027) is the genuinely unpriced, label-defining binary; magnitude high, but the nearest-dated leg (2H26 EU approval) is the lower-impact one.
verified.primary_source: NewAmsterdam Pharma 2026 Strategic Priorities, GlobeNewswire 2026-01-09 (EMA decision 2H26); EMA MAA acceptance, Menarini/PRNewswire 2025; PREVAIL interim 4Q2026 / topline 1Q2027 per company corporate updates 2026
verified.analysis: The triage note's "EU-only, US path unclear" framing is accurate on the regulatory leg but undersells the setup: the dominant value driver is PREVAIL (interim 4Q2026, topline 1Q2027), the CV-outcomes readout that separates obicetrapib from a pure LDL-lowering label. The 2H26 EMA/MHRA/Swissmedic decision is real, dated, and forward (unfired), with Menarini as named counterparty and Q4 EU launches contingent on approval. US FDA NDA timing is genuinely undated in all 2026 filings/PRs — a confirmed gap, so the near-term catalyst is EU + the PREVAIL clinical binary rather than a US regulatory event.

verified.catalyst: NCR Atleos is being acquired by Brink's (BCO) for $30 cash + 0.1574 BCO/share (announced Feb 26, 2026), a live cash-and-stock merger-arb spread.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder special meetings vote 2026-06-30 (tomorrow); deal expected to close Q1 2027, outside date extendable to Aug 26, 2027 for regulatory approvals.
verified.instrument: NATL common (arb vs short 0.1574 BCO to isolate the cash leg / capture spread)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Brink's/BCO), G2 (signed board-recommended merger agreement with $4.5B committed bridge financing), G3 (live ~$1.62 / ~3.8% gross spread to a defined $44.50 implied value)
verified.edge: M — spread is real and dated, but BCO has fallen ~29% since announcement ($129.58 to $92.16), shrinking implied value from $50.40 to ~$44.50; the cash leg is fixed at $30 but the stock stub carries BCO equity risk unless hedged short, and shareholder suits / Q1'27 regulatory timing add deal risk reflected in NATL's discount to implied value.
verified.primary_source: NCR Atleos investor relations / SEC Form 8-K & S-4/A: "Brink's to Acquire NCR Atleos for $6.6 Billion" (announced 2026-02-26); shareholder meetings/supplemental disclosures reaffirmed June 2026; quotes via FMP 2026-06-29 (NATL $42.88, BCO $92.16).
verified.kill_fact: Every load-bearing fact verified against primary sources. SEC DEFM14A/425 & Feb 26 2026 8-K: signed two-step merger, $30.00 cash + 0.1574 BCO/share, board-recommended; NATL+BCO special meetings BOTH 6/30/26 (vote tomorrow, as stated); outside date Feb 26 2027 auto-extending to Aug 26 2027 (matches 'extendable Aug-2027'). Live FMP: NATL $42.88, BCO $92.16. Spread math exact: $30 + 0.1574*92.16 = $44.50 implied vs $42.88 = $1.62 gross (~3.8%). BCO erosion real (announce-day $129.58 implied $50.40 -> now $44.50), validating the score-7 cap for stub equity risk + unhedged-leg exposure. Forward, signed, non-macro arb — not fired, not stale, terms/dates/magnitude all true. Minor overlay only: shareholder suits/proxy scrutiny, deal reaffirmed.
verified.analysis: Confirmed live, signed cash-and-stock merger: $30 cash + 0.1574 BCO per NATL share, board-recommended, financing committed, with a dated shareholder vote 6/30/26 and Q1'27 expected close. At BCO $92.16 the deal implies ~$44.50/share vs NATL $42.88, a real ~$1.62 (~3.8%) gross spread (~6% annualized to close) that is not yet priced in — a clean forward, non-macro arb. Score capped at 7 (not higher) because BCO's sharp post-announcement decline has eroded headline value and injected equity-market risk into the unhedged stub, and the persistent discount signals the market assigns nontrivial deal/timing risk through the extendable Aug-2027 outside date.

verified.catalyst: NioCorp is targeting binding EXIM debt commitments (~$780-800M, of a ~$1.1-1.2B project) to reach Elk Creek bankability, which would in turn trigger a definitive Stellantis rare-earth offtake and unlock construction.
verified.dated_milestone: Targeted EXIM financing close / 65% debt commitment in Q2 2026 (company guidance per May 2026 deck); next gate = completion of EXIM TRC-2/TRC-3 due diligence (SLR technical+environmental review ongoing). No fixed dated trigger — target is soft and has slipped since the Apr-2024 preliminary term sheet.
verified.instrument: equity (NB common); thinly-financed pre-revenue developer, high binary dilution risk on equity portion of the 65/35 structure
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: EXIM Bank, Stellantis, DoD/DPA Title III, J.P. Morgan as arranger) and G2 (concrete commitment in process: ~$780-800M EXIM debt application advanced to independent technical review). G3 fails: no specific currently-unpriced figure with a hard date — the Q2'26 close is a soft company target, not a contracted event.
verified.edge: M — real named counterparties and a sizeable financing in active EXIM due diligence, but edge is capped because (a) the Q2'26 close is a repeatedly-slipped soft target not a hard trigger, (b) the Stellantis "definitive" agreement is still the July-2023 non-binding term sheet, explicitly conditional on financing, and (c) outcome hinges on a government bank's discretionary authorization, which carries a macro/policy-bet flavor.
verified.primary_source: NioCorp/EXIM disclosures: "EXIM Advances NioCorp Elk Creek to Independent Technical Review" (niocorp.com) + mining.com EXIM due-diligence update (2025-10-02) + Stellantis-NioCorp rare-earth offtake term sheet (2023-07-07, non-binding) + AInvest "Q2 2026 Bank Financing Hurdle" + NioCorp May-2026 investor presentation; verified 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real and specific — EXIM has advanced NioCorp's ~$780-800M loan to TRC-2/TRC-3 independent technical+environmental review (SLR Consulting), J.P. Morgan is engaged as arranger, and a definitive Stellantis offtake plus DoD DPA Title III work sit downstream of a financing close the company guides to Q2 2026. But none of it has fired: the Stellantis agreement remains a July-2023 non-binding term sheet conditional on project finance, and the EXIM close is a soft target that has slipped since the April-2024 preliminary term sheet through ~2.5 years of due diligence, with final authorization at a government bank's discretion. It clears Bloom G1/G2 but not G3 (no contracted, hard-dated, currently-unpriced figure), and the discretionary-government-financing dependency gives it a partial macro/policy-bet character — hence WATCH not ACTIVE, score 5.
